data.body:
=== DESIGN DOC: our-review-dimensions-v2.md ===

# rebar plan-review gate — consolidated design v2 (extensible dimension registry)

Supersedes our-review-dimensions.md. Folds both DSO gap reports + the polyglot-tooling research.
Full DSO citations: dso-dimension-catalog-v2.md. Fires at open→in_progress; `claim` = fast signed-attestation
check; heavy review out-of-band; REVIEW PROCESS not dialog (client revises + re-runs to earn a fresh signature).

================================================================
## 0 — CORE ARCHITECTURE PRINCIPLE: designed to GROW (per Joe)
================================================================
Tuning / adding / removing a dimension must be **trivial and reliable**. Therefore a dimension is a
**first-class declarative descriptor in a registry**, not bespoke code. The orchestrator is generic; it never
names a specific dimension.

**Dimension descriptor (the unit of extension):**
- `id`, `name`, `scenario(s)`
- `kind`: sound | judgment
- `exec`: DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP | AGENT
- `routing`: base (always-on) | container-branch (has_children) | leaf-branch (no children) | overlay (triggered)
- `trigger` (overlays only): router-LLM relevance criteria + deterministic signal priors
- `tools` (DET/AGENT): which deterministic tools prime it (solidlsp / ast-grep / syft / scc / lizard)
- `impl`: a DET function ref OR an LLM prompt-file ref (+ runner)
- `contract`: structured output schema — uniform verdict {PASS|AMBIGUOUS|FAIL} + findings[] (each cited)
- `block_policy`: block | escalate | advise (per-dim; see §1) + measured-FP gate
- `config`: tunable thresholds (counts, etc.)

**Reuse rebar's EXISTING extension points** — do NOT invent a plugin system. rebar.llm already has the exact
pattern: reviewer `catalog.json` entry + `contracts.py` schema + prompt file + pluggable `runner`. Each
judgment dimension = a catalog entry + contract + prompt; each DET dimension = a check module behind the same
descriptor + verdict contract. Add/remove/tune = edit the registry + drop a prompt/check file. No orchestrator
change. Schema-validated output makes it reliable.

**Consequence (de-risks the v1 cut):** dimensions are ADDITIVE. v1 ships a core registry; everything deferred
is added later by registering a descriptor — no rework. So the v1/deferred line is low-stakes and movable.

**FUTURE SEAM (design-for, do NOT implement this epic):** project-specific review criteria. Same descriptor
format, loaded from project config (`.rebar/`). GAP-6 "project-invariant compliance" (rebar's own module-size /
strangler / no-files-<100-LOC policies) is the first natural project-supplied dimension. We design the
descriptor + registry so this slots in later; we ship only built-in dimensions now.

================================================================
## 1 — SHARED BASE: the Anti-FP operating contract (every judgment dim inherits)
================================================================
This is the "reviewer-base" of our system — the base class. Because dimensions are pluggable, making this the
SHARED BASE means every new dimension inherits low-FP behavior for free (this is *why* growth stays reliable).
- **cite-or-omit (hard gate):** a FAIL/AMBIGUOUS finding without a verbatim file:line citation is auto-dropped.
- **reachable-harm bar:** a finding only BLOCKS if it names the concrete in-session execution path to a wrong
  outcome; otherwise → AMBIGUOUS. (Unverified claim cannot be top severity.)
- **severity MINOR-floor + NOT-flag auto-downgrades** (DSO reviewer-base lists).
- **single-question scoping:** each dimension answers ONLY its own question; cross-dim findings out of scope.
- **behavioral-test carve-outs** (DSO 7 VALID patterns) — esp. **Rule-5 structural-greps + command-output
  greps**, CRITICAL because rebar is heavily bash/instruction-files and would self-false-flag otherwise.
- **blue-team adjudicator** pass suppresses over-critical FPs (red surfaces → blue filters).
- **AMBIGUOUS is the de-escalation default** ("prefer AMBIGUOUS+question over FAIL+handwave").
- **per-dimension dismissal-rate self-calibration:** measure each dim's downstream dismissal; >threshold ⇒
  over-triggering ⇒ auto-throttle / demote from blocking. This is the data that earns a judgment dim the block tier.
VERDICT MODEL: per-dim PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL. AMBIGUOUS = non-blocking → escalates to higher-tier adjudicator.
FAIL→block is per-dim (block_policy), NOT deterministic-only; a judgment dim MAY block once its measured FP
clears the bar. Abstain trigger = calibrated multi-sample agreement, never raw confidence.

================================================================
## 2 — DETERMINISTIC TOOLING (fail-open contract)
================================================================
Semantic core (resolution/callers) = **solidlsp (Serena's LSP lib, ALREADY in this repo)** + ast-grep backstop.
Deps = **syft** (enumerate) + thin per-ecosystem registry HTTP (existence) + LLM-fallback long tail.
Size = **scc** (+ lizard for real CCN). Locus/structural = **ast-grep / tree-sitter**.
FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT: capability map; unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout → `skipped` = PASS, never a
violation; hard "missing" only from top-tier servers (pyright/gopls/rust-analyzer/tsserver/clangd), weak server
→ "could-not-confirm". Avoid stack-graphs (archived) + Glean (over-engineered); osv-scanner ≠ existence oracle.

================================================================
## 3 — DIMENSION REGISTRY (consolidated; v1 = ships first wave)
================================================================
### Tier 0 — DET prefilter (fail-open)
- P1 Readiness shape (standardized `## Acceptance Criteria` all types) · base · DET · v1
- P2 File/symbol/import resolution · solidlsp+ast-grep · DET · v1
- P3 Package existence · syft+registry+LLM-fallback · DET · v1
- P4 Oversize signals (AC/file_impact counts, conjunction/CRUD verbs, layers/interfaces; scc/lizard) · config-tunable · DET · v1
- P5 Task-DAG validity + finer cross-task interference (shared file/symbol/DB-table/config-key, output-format assumptions) · solidlsp/ast-grep · DET · v1
- P6 **AC/DD quality (NEW)**: compound-AND split · 9-word vague lexicon · spec-fidelity ≥80% word-overlap · **verify-command robustness** (grep-anchor/shell-escape/argv-vs-help/cardinality-guard) · DET · v1
- P7 Destructive/irreversible sniff → escalation to T4 (never pass/fail) · DET · v1

### Tier 1 — judgment, 1-TURN / 2-STEP (cheap, abstain-capable; inherit §1)
- F1 Criteria measurability & **agent-in-session-completability** (+ verify-cmd judgment remainder: wrong-assertion-target/fixture-validity) · 1-TURN · v1
- F4 User/problem present (light content check; understanding-gate content) · 1-TURN · v1
- E1 Criteria↔description coherence + **every-criterion-maps-to-a-step** · 1-TURN · v1
- E2 Ambiguity / executable-without-clarification (+ spike/research/TBD = undercooked) · 1-TURN · v1
- E3 Intent fidelity · 2-STEP (blind-restate → compare; child-vs-slice) · v1
- E5 Testing-plan completeness (behavioral+contractual gaps, change-detector avoidance, **failure-path + boundary coverage**, **RED-before-GREEN ordering**) · 1-TURN · v1
- E6 Verification/termination completeness (+ per-step codebase-green/testable-behavior atomicity, RED→impl edge) · 1-TURN · v1
- G5 Decomposition judgment (too-big/should-have-children; + **vertical-slice vs horizontal-layer**; consumes P4) · 1-TURN · v1

### Tier 2 — judgment, AGENT (tool-grounded, primed by DET tools, fail-open; inherit §1)
- E4 **Assumption/premise verification (MERGED E4+E9, NEW — biggest gap)**: DET hedge/assertion phrase-scan → **forced codebase probe** on each named source (ban cached-context reasoning) + blue-team · AGENT · v1
- G1+G2 Edit-set/scope accuracy: (1) edit-target first (modify-existing=verify locus / create-new=verify placement) → (2) deterministic consumer/reference lookup (solidlsp find-references) incl. **rename/deprecate enumeration completeness**; hallucinated+missing; high-blast≠fail · AGENT · v1
- G3 Child coverage — **4-bucket audit** (fully/partial/uncovered/structural) + **verbatim 3-condition standard** + mandatory coverage map · AGENT · v1 (container)
- G4 Child consistency — **all 7 categories** (implicit_shared_state/conflicting_assumptions/dependency_gap/scope_overlap/ordering/consumer_impact/residual_refs) · AGENT · v1 (container)
- A1 NIH / config-surface-proliferation anti-patterns (codebase-grounded) + Rule-of-Three call-site count · AGENT · DEFERRED

### Tier 3 — triggered overlays (router-LLM selects relevance; overlay judges adequacy)
- T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification (+ anti-repackaging, + Rule-of-Three) · AGENT(web) · v1
- T2 Empirical probe (red→green / spike) · AGENT · v1
- T3 Integration feasibility (+ CLI --help subcommand-inventory + assumed-vs-actual diff + auth-HTTPS precond + deprecated-API) · AGENT · v1
- T4 Compat/destructiveness as EXPLICIT JUSTIFIED CHOICE — bidirectional (unacknowledged breakage AND gratuitous compat/flags) + expand-contract sequencing + rollback-path · 1-TURN/AGENT · v1
- T5a Performance / T5b Reliability (blast-radius-only-lowers tiebreaker) / T5c Security (ambiguity-not-absence) / T5d Accessibility / **T5e Maintainability (coupling_risk + changeability, NEW)** · 1-TURN default · DEFERRED (2nd wave)
- T6 UX non-happy-path · 1-TURN · DEFERRED
- T7 Documentation (new-needed + existing-invalidated + not-excessive/non-navigable + hot-path-bloat) · 1-TURN · DEFERRED
- T8 LLM/prompt antipatterns (bot-psychologist 17-pt) · 1-TURN/AGENT · DEFERRED
- T9 **Shared-state lifecycle (NEW)**: CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE · AGENT · DEFERRED

### Cross-cutting machinery
- Router-LLM triage (selects overlays; DET signals as priors; bias to recall)
- Blue-team adjudicator (FP filter) · verdict aggregation · attestation signing + invalidation-on-edit
- Project-invariant compliance (GAP-6) · config-tunable · **FUTURE SEAM, not v1**

================================================================
## 4 — OUT OF SCOPE (confirmed)
================================================================
- F2 no_overlap / F3 dependency_aware (cross-ticket): deterministic-now (reuse next_batch file-impact overlap +
  explicit depends_on/blocks link presence); SEMANTIC backlog-scan duplication → follow-on.
- Propose-multiple-then-select loop (authoring, not review). Cross-service deploy topology (not in-session-verifiable).
- DSO validation_signal's dogfooding/staged-rollout/7-day-monitor — VIOLATES agent-completable-in-session; F1 rejects.
- DSO all-equal-5 / 1-5 scoring rigidity — adopt dimension VALUES, shed the gating. E8 test-gaming → code-review/close gate.
- Project-specific dimension loading — design the seam, implement later.

================================================================
## 5 — OPEN DECISIONS
================================================================
1. Anti-FP operating contract (§1) as binding shared base — RECOMMEND YES (it's also what makes growth reliable).
2. v1 cut (§3 v1 flags) — now low-stakes (additive). Confirm or adjust.
3. Per-dimension block_policy (block/escalate/advise) — Joe's deferred call; the dismissal-rate self-calibration
   (§1) is the data that earns a judgment dim the block tier.
